description: Russia launched a massive wave of approximately 430 drones and 68 missiles targeting energy infrastructure and civilian areas across at least six Ukrainian regions.
news_context:
The attack was part of Russia's intensified campaign during the winter and spring to cripple Ukraine's energy grid and weaken civilian morale. President Zelenskyy stated the primary target was the energy infrastructure in the Kyiv region, but that residential buildings and schools were also hit.

The strikes are a continuation of a tactic used by Russia throughout the war to inflict maximum disruption on Ukrainian life far from the front lines. The large-scale, coordinated attack across multiple regions demonstrates a strategic effort to overwhelm Ukrainian air defenses and cause widespread power outages as ground forces continue their offensive in the east.

description: An Israeli airstrike hit a house in the Nuseirat refugee camp, killing four people, including a couple, their 10-year-old son, and a 15-year-old neighbor.
news_context: The strike occurred amid ongoing, almost daily Israeli attacks in the Gaza Strip despite a ceasefire agreement brokered in October. This event is part of a pattern of Israeli military actions targeting residential areas in Gaza, which Israel often justifies by claiming to target Hamas assets, though such claims were not immediately made for this specific strike.

description: An Israeli airstrike targeted a vehicle carrying police officers on Salah al-Din Street in central Gaza, killing nine officers, including a senior official.
news_context: The strike targeted members of the Hamas-run police force, which had begun to re-emerge in areas not controlled by the Israeli military following the October ceasefire. Israel has consistently targeted Hamas security forces throughout the conflict, viewing them as part of the militant group's governing infrastructure.

description: An Israeli airstrike destroyed a primary healthcare facility belonging to the Islamic Health Authority in the southern Lebanese village of Burj Qalaouiyah, killing 12 medical workers.
news_context: This attack was part of a significant escalation in Israeli strikes against Lebanon, which have increasingly targeted civilian and public infrastructure. The Lebanese Ministry of Health stated the attack "violated all international humanitarian laws" and brought the total number of medical staff killed by Israeli strikes in the preceding 12 days to 31.

description: A series of Israeli airstrikes across southern and eastern Lebanon killed at least 34 people and wounded dozens more in towns including Baalbek, Nabatieh, and Tyre.
news_context: The strikes represent a major escalation in the cross-border conflict that began on March 2nd. The attack in Baalbek, deep inside Lebanese territory, targeted a residential building, killing a Syrian family. These attacks are part of Israel's widespread bombing campaign across Lebanon in response to Hezbollah rocket fire, which has displaced nearly one million people.

description: The U.S. Africa Command conducted a precision airstrike targeting ISIS-Somalia militants in the Golis Mountains, approximately 70 km southeast of Bossaso.
news_context: This strike was conducted in coordination with the Federal Government of Somalia as part of ongoing efforts to degrade the capabilities of ISIS-Somalia. U.S. airstrikes in Somalia have increased significantly under the current administration, targeting both ISIS-Somalia and al-Shabaab, even as local Somali forces have scaled back some ground operations in the targeted region.

description: Armed militants on motorcycles launched coordinated attacks on a military drone base and a battalion headquarters in Tahoua (Air Base 401), but were repelled by Nigerien forces.
news_context: The attack occurs amid a backdrop of persistent jihadist violence in Niger and the wider Sahel region, perpetrated by groups affiliated with Al-Qaeda and the Islamic State. While no group immediately claimed responsibility, the incident follows a recent attack claimed by the Islamic State on the main airport in the capital, Niamey, highlighting the ongoing security challenges for the military junta that took power in July 2023.

description: Ukrainian forces used ATACMS and SCALP missiles to strike garages and a warehouse at Donetsk Airport used by Russian forces to store and operate strike drones.
news_context: The strike targets a key logistical and operational hub for Russia's drone warfare efforts in the occupied Donetsk region. This attack is part of Ukraine's broader strategy to degrade Russian military capabilities by striking high-value assets far behind the front lines, disrupting their ability to conduct aerial attacks.

target_name: Multiple Regions — Civilian and Infrastructure Targets
description: Russia launched 126 UAVs of various types (including Shahed, Gerbera, and Italmas) alongside an Iskander-M ballistic missile overnight in Ukraine. Air defenses neutralized 117 of the drones.
news_context: This attack is part of Russia's ongoing aerial campaign against Ukraine, which frequently employs large numbers of drones to overwhelm air defenses and strike various targets. Ukrainian air defenses reported intercepting or suppressing 117 of the drones, but the hits that did occur add to the cumulative damage inflicted on Ukrainian infrastructure and civilian areas.

description: An Israeli airstrike on a group of people in the Shuja'iyya neighborhood east of Gaza City killed three Palestinians, including two boys.
news_context: This strike adds to the civilian death toll in Gaza during a period of supposed ceasefire. Attacks in densely populated neighborhoods like Shuja'iyya have been a recurring feature of the conflict, drawing condemnation from international bodies due to the high risk to non-combatants.

description: An Israeli airstrike completely destroyed a four-story residential building in Baalbek, killing five people and injuring 15.
news_context: This attack occurred deep within Lebanese territory, far from the southern border area where clashes are more frequent, signifying a major escalation in the conflict. The strike is part of a broader Israeli air campaign against targets across Lebanon that began in earnest on March 2, 2026, following Hezbollah rocket attacks.

description: Jihadist militants attacked a specialized police unit (GUMI) in Yamba, near Fada N'gourma, killing at least 12 officers.
news_context: The attack is part of a surge in violence attributed to the Al-Qaeda affiliated Group for the Support of Islam and Muslims (JNIM). This specific raid on a police unit in the restive eastern part of the country reflects the ongoing inability of the military junta to contain the expanding jihadist insurgency that has plagued Burkina Faso and the wider Sahel region for years.

target_name: Nuseirat Refugee Camp — Displaced Persons' Tents
description: An Israeli drone strike targeted tents sheltering displaced families in the Nuseirat refugee camp, killing two women and injuring seven others.
news_context: The attack highlights the extreme vulnerability of displaced populations within the Gaza Strip, who are often forced to shelter in makeshift camps. This event occurred amid ongoing Israeli military operations that have continued despite a formal ceasefire, with attacks frequently reported in and around designated safe zones and refugee camps.

description: Russian forces launched 97 attack drones of various types into Ukraine, with Ukrainian air defenses downing or suppressing 90 of them.
news_context: This attack followed a massive missile and drone barrage from the previous night, continuing Russia's strategy of persistent, large-scale aerial assaults. The use of multiple drone types, including Shahed, Gerbera, and Italmas, is intended to probe and overwhelm Ukraine's integrated air defense system across a wide geographic area.

description: Israeli airstrikes on the towns of Majdal Selm, Aaitit, and Qatrani in southern Lebanon killed a total of 10 people and wounded 13 others.
news_context: These strikes are part of the ongoing, escalating conflict between Israel and Hezbollah that began on March 2nd. The attacks contribute to a rising death toll in Lebanon, which the country's health ministry reported has surpassed 850 since the start of the month, with a notable number of child casualties.

description: An Israeli drone strike targeted a Hamas office in the Dahieh neighborhood of Beirut, killing senior Hamas official Saleh al-Arouri and six others.
news_context:
The assassination of Saleh al-Arouri marked a significant escalation in the ongoing conflict following the October 7 attacks. Al-Arouri was the deputy chairman of Hamas's political bureau and a key figure in coordinating with Hezbollah and Iran.

The strike in Beirut's southern suburbs, a Hezbollah stronghold, raised fears of a broader regional war. It was the first Israeli strike on the Lebanese capital since 2006, crossing a red line established by Hezbollah leadership regarding attacks within Beirut.

description: Two suicide bombers detonated explosives during a memorial procession for Qasem Soleimani in Kerman, killing 94 people and injuring over 280.
news_context:
The Islamic State (ISIS) claimed responsibility for the dual suicide bombings, stating they were targeting Shia Muslims whom they consider apostates. The attack took place on the fourth anniversary of the assassination of Qasem Soleimani, the former commander of the IRGC Quds Force.

The bombings represented the deadliest terrorist attack in Iran since the 1979 Islamic Revolution. It occurred amidst heightened regional tensions, prompting Iranian authorities to vow harsh retaliation and increased security measures along its borders with Afghanistan and Pakistan.

description: A US drone strike targeted a Popular Mobilization Forces logistics facility in Baghdad, killing Harakat Hezbollah al-Nujaba commander Mushtaq.
news_context:
The US military stated the strike was taken in self-defense against a leader who was actively planning and directing attacks against American personnel in Iraq and Syria. The targeted group, Harakat Hezbollah al-Nujaba, is an Iran-backed militia operating under the umbrella of the PMF.

The Iraqi government strongly condemned the strike as an unprovoked violation of its sovereignty, further straining relations between Washington and Baghdad. It prompted renewed calls from Iraqi political factions to expel US-led coalition forces from the country.

target_name: Mount Meron — Air Traffic Control Base
description: Hezbollah fired over 40 rockets at the Israeli military's Mount Meron air traffic control base, causing significant damage to radar installations.
news_context:
The group described the attack as an "initial response" to the assassination of senior Hamas leader Saleh al-Arouri in Beirut days earlier. The Mount Meron base is a highly sensitive and strategic asset responsible for coordinating Israeli air operations across Lebanon and Syria.

The IDF confirmed that two of the base's radar radomes were damaged but stated that backup systems ensured no disruption to operational capabilities. The strike marked a significant escalation in the caliber of targets selected by Hezbollah.

description: An Israeli drone strike hit an SUV in the southern Lebanese village of Khirbet Selm, killing Wissam al-Tawil, a senior commander of Hezbollah.
news_context:
Al-Tawil was the highest-ranking Hezbollah official killed by Israel since the skirmishes began in October. He played a central role in directing Hezbollah's operations in the south, including the recent strike on the Mount Meron airbase.

His targeted assassination further inflamed tensions, with Hezbollah vowing revenge and launching a retaliatory drone swarm at the IDF Northern Command headquarters in Safed the following day. The targeted killings formed a key part of Israel's strategy to push Radwan forces away from the border.

description: US and UK forces conducted widespread airstrikes and Tomahawk missile strikes against dozens of Houthi targets across Yemen, including radar sites.
news_context:
The strikes were launched in response to repeated Houthi attacks on international commercial shipping in the Red Sea and Gulf of Aden. The US-led coalition had issued multiple warnings to the Iran-backed group, culminating in a UN Security Council resolution demanding a halt to the maritime attacks.

This operation marked a significant internationalization of the conflict, as Western powers intervened directly against Houthi military infrastructure. Despite the strikes, Houthi leadership remained defiant, vowing to continue their blockade in solidarity with Palestinians in Gaza.

description: Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps fired ballistic missiles at a residence in Erbil, killing a Kurdish businessman and three family members.
news_context:
Iran claimed the strike targeted a Mossad espionage center involved in planning attacks within Iranian territory. However, Kurdish and Iraqi officials strongly denied any Israeli intelligence presence at the targeted civilian home, condemning the attack as a blatant violation of sovereignty.

The strike was part of a broader series of missile launches by Iran targeting alleged threats in Iraq and Syria in the aftermath of the Kerman bombings. The action drew widespread international condemnation and led Iraq to recall its ambassador from Tehran.

description: Iranian forces launched missile and drone strikes against two alleged bases of the Sunni militant group Jaish al-Adl in Pakistan, killing two.
news_context:
Iran stated the preemptive strikes targeted terrorists who were preparing cross-border attacks into Iranian territory. Jaish al-Adl has been responsible for numerous deadly attacks against Iranian security forces in the Sistan and Baluchestan province over the years.

The unprecedented cross-border strike infuriated the Pakistani government, which denounced it as an unprovoked violation of its airspace. In response, Pakistan recalled its ambassador from Tehran and expelled the Iranian ambassador, triggering a brief but sharp diplomatic crisis.

description: The Pakistani Air Force conducted precision airstrikes on militant hideouts in Iran's Sistan and Baluchestan province, killing nine people.
news_context:
Codenamed Operation Marg Bar Sarmachar, the strikes targeted bases used by the Balochistan Liberation Army (BLA) and Balochistan Liberation Front (BLF). Pakistan claimed the action was necessary due to a lack of Iranian action against safe havens used by these separatist groups.

The airstrikes were a direct, tit-for-tat retaliation for Iran's cross-border attack two days earlier. Despite the military exchange, both nations subsequently moved quickly to de-escalate tensions and resume normal diplomatic relations by the end of the month.

target_name: Al Asad Airbase — US Military Facilities
description: Iran-backed militias launched a heavy barrage of ballistic missiles and rockets at the Al Asad Airbase in Iraq, injuring several US personnel.
news_context:
The Islamic Resistance in Iraq, an umbrella group of Iran-backed militias, claimed the attack as part of their campaign to force the withdrawal of US troops from the region. The assault was one of the largest and most intense bombardments against US forces since the Israel-Hamas war began.

While Patriot air defense systems intercepted most of the projectiles, some bypassed the defenses and impacted the base. The sustained attacks highlighted the vulnerability of US outposts and increased pressure on Washington to formulate a stronger deterrent strategy.

description: An Israeli airstrike destroyed a multi-story building in the Mezzeh neighborhood of Damascus, killing five senior IRGC officers.
news_context:
The targeted building was reportedly used as a covert command center by the IRGC to coordinate arms transfers to Hezbollah and other regional proxies. Israel has conducted a long-running shadow war targeting Iranian entrenchment in Syria, but significantly escalated assassinations following October 7.

Iran's President Ebrahim Raisi condemned the attack and vowed retaliation against Israel. The precise intelligence required to conduct the strike suggested a deep infiltration of Iranian networks in Syria, further destabilizing the fragile security environment in the capital.

description: Houthi forces fired three anti-ship ballistic missiles at the US-flagged container ship Maersk Detroit in the Gulf of Aden, but all missed.
news_context:
The Maersk Detroit and its sister ship were carrying cargo for the US Department of Defense and USAID, escorted by US Navy warships. The attack demonstrated the Houthis' willingness to directly target vessels affiliated with the US government.

Following the near-miss, Maersk suspended all transits through the Red Sea for its US-flagged subsidiary, dealing a significant blow to military logistics routes. The US Navy subsequently intensified its preemptive strikes on Houthi launch sites in Yemen.

description: A one-way attack drone struck a US logistics support base known as Tower 22 in northeastern Jordan, killing three American service members.
news_context:
The drone managed to bypass air defenses by trailing a returning US drone, striking the living quarters early in the morning. This marked the first time US troops were killed by hostile fire in the Middle East since the outbreak of the Gaza war.

The attack triggered widespread outrage in Washington and forced the Biden administration to authorize a major retaliatory campaign. The Islamic Resistance in Iraq claimed responsibility, though Iran formally denied direct involvement in ordering the strike.

description: The USS Gravely shot down a Houthi anti-ship cruise missile using its Phalanx weapon system when the projectile came within one mile of the ship.
news_context:
This incident marked the closest a Houthi missile had come to striking a US warship since the crisis began, bypassing the vessel's longer-range SM-2 defense layers. The reliance on the last line of defense highlighted the increasing sophistication and volume of Houthi attacks.

The engagement raised alarms in the Pentagon regarding the sustainability of the naval defense mission and the potential for a catastrophic hit on an American destroyer. It prompted adjustments in defensive postures for coalition vessels operating in the Red Sea.

description: Multiple Ukrainian MAGURA V5 uncrewed surface vessels struck the Russian missile corvette Ivanovets in Lake Donuzlav, causing it to sink.
news_context:
The audacious nighttime operation was conducted by Group 13, a specialized unit of Ukraine's Main Directorate of Intelligence (HUR). Video footage showed the naval drones repeatedly hitting the vessel's hull, culminating in the detonation of its anti-ship missiles.

The sinking of the Ivanovets further degraded the combat capabilities of the Russian Black Sea Fleet, which had been forced to largely retreat from occupied Crimea. It demonstrated Ukraine's innovative and highly successful asymmetrical naval warfare strategy.

description: US bombers struck 85 targets across Iraq and Syria linked to the IRGC Quds Force and affiliated militias in retaliation for the Tower 22 attack.
news_context:
The massive aerial bombardment was the direct response to the deadly Tower 22 drone attack that killed three American soldiers in Jordan. Targets included command centers, intelligence hubs, and weapons storage facilities used to supply militant groups.

The operation was designed to severely degrade the operational capabilities of Iran-backed networks without sparking a direct war with Tehran. Both the Iraqi and Syrian governments condemned the strikes as violations of sovereignty, reporting multiple casualties among security personnel and civilians.

description: A US drone fired a Hellfire R9X missile at a moving vehicle in eastern Baghdad, assassinating Kata'ib Hezbollah senior commander Abu Baqir.
news_context:
Al-Saadi was responsible for directing Kata'ib Hezbollah's operations in Syria and was directly linked by the Pentagon to the planning of the deadly Tower 22 drone attack in Jordan. The precision strike used a specialized non-explosive blade munition to minimize civilian casualties.

The assassination in the heart of the Iraqi capital infuriated the Iraqi government, which labeled it a reckless breach of sovereignty. It massively accelerated political efforts by Shia factions to formally expel the US-led international coalition from Iraqi territory.

description: Ukrainian MAGURA V5 naval drones repeatedly struck the Russian landing ship Caesar Kunikov off the coast of Alupka, causing it to capsize and sink.
news_context:
The attack occurred on the anniversary of the death of the Soviet officer for whom the ship was named. The vessel was reportedly transporting military cargo from Novorossiysk to supply Russian forces in the occupied Crimean peninsula.

The loss of another large landing ship severely complicated Russian logistics across the Black Sea and Sea of Azov. Following the sinking, the commander of the Russian Black Sea Fleet, Admiral Viktor Sokolov, was reportedly dismissed from his post.

description: An Israeli airstrike targeted a multi-story residential building in the southern Lebanese city of Nabatieh, killing 10 civilians.
news_context:
The IDF stated the strike intended to target a Hezbollah commander, Ali al-Debs, who was killed in the attack alongside two other fighters. However, the high civilian toll sparked fierce international condemnation and accusations of disproportionate use of force.

The Lebanese government filed an urgent complaint with the UN Security Council, calling the attack a "massacre." The strike in Nabatieh, deep within Lebanon, marked a dangerous expansion of the conflict zones that had previously been confined to border villages.

description: Houthi militants fired two anti-ship ballistic missiles at the Belize-flagged cargo vessel Rubymar in the Bab el-Mandeb Strait.
news_context:
The attack resulted in the crew abandoning ship, marking one of the most successful Houthi strikes on commercial shipping to date. The Rubymar was carrying thousands of tons of fertilizer, leading to severe environmental concerns in the Red Sea.

Despite heavy US and UK airstrikes attempting to degrade their capabilities, the Houthis demonstrated their continued ability to threaten global supply chains. The vessel ultimately sank weeks later, becoming the first ship fully destroyed in the Houthi maritime campaign.

description: Israeli warplanes launched airstrikes near the eastern Lebanese city of Baalbek, killing two Hezbollah members in a major regional escalation.
news_context:
The strikes targeted air defense infrastructure deep inside Lebanese territory, approximately 100 kilometers from the Israeli border. Israel framed the attack as direct retaliation for Hezbollah shooting down an advanced Hermes 450 drone over southern Lebanon earlier that day.

Striking the Bekaa Valley, a critical Hezbollah logistical stronghold near the Syrian border, represented a major escalation. Hezbollah immediately responded by firing a heavy barrage of 60 Katyusha rockets at an Israeli army base in the Golan Heights.

description: Israeli forces opened fire on a massive crowd of Palestinians gathered around a humanitarian aid convoy at the Al-Nabulsi roundabout, killing 118.
news_context:
The event, dubbed the "Flour Massacre," occurred as thousands of starving civilians rushed aid trucks arriving in northern Gaza. The IDF claimed troops fired only warning shots and targeted "suspects" approaching tanks, attributing most deaths to a deadly stampede and individuals being run over by trucks.

Palestinian health officials and numerous eyewitnesses stated that Israeli troops directly fired heavy machine guns into the dense crowd. The horrific incident triggered severe global backlash, prompting the US and other nations to urgently begin air-dropping aid and plan a temporary maritime pier to bypass Israeli land bottlenecks.

description: A Russian Shahed-136 drone crashed into a nine-story residential building in Odesa, causing a partial collapse that killed 12 civilians.
news_context:
The overnight drone swarm targeted civilian infrastructure in southern Ukraine, bypassing local air defenses. Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy used the tragedy to urgently call on Western allies to provide more advanced air defense systems and ammunition.

The strike highlighted Russia's tactic of using cheap, Iranian-designed loitering munitions to deplete Ukrainian air defenses and terrorize civilian populations far from the frontlines. Search and rescue operations lasted for two days as workers cleared the massive rubble pile.

description: A Ukrainian long-range attack drone struck a five-story apartment building in St. Petersburg, heavily damaging the facade.
news_context:
The drone was allegedly targeting the nearby Ruchi oil depot but crashed into the residential block after being intercepted or malfunctioning. It demonstrated Ukraine's expanding capability to strike strategic targets deep within Russian territory, nearly 1,000 kilometers from the border.

The attack on Russia's second-largest city and President Putin's hometown brought the reality of the war closer to the Russian urban populace. Ukraine deliberately escalated its campaign against Russian energy and industrial infrastructure throughout early 2024 to disrupt military logistics.

description: Ukrainian MAGURA V5 naval drones attacked and sank the Russian Project 22160 patrol ship Sergey Kotov near the Kerch Strait, killing 7 crew.
news_context:
The modern patrol vessel, valued at $65 million, had played a role in the attack on Snake Island early in the war. The coordinated nighttime swarm attack hit the ship's stern, starboard, and port sides, causing catastrophic flooding.

The sinking occurred dangerously close to the Crimean Bridge, temporarily halting traffic on the strategic span. It further marginalized the Russian Black Sea Fleet, validating Ukraine's strategy of utilizing cost-effective USVs to achieve sea denial without a traditional navy.

description: A Houthi anti-ship ballistic missile struck the Barbados-flagged bulk carrier True Confidence in the Gulf of Aden, killing three crew members.
news_context:
The strike resulted in the first confirmed civilian fatalities of the Houthi campaign against commercial shipping. The crew was forced to abandon the severely damaged and burning vessel, prompting rescue operations by nearby coalition naval forces.

The lethal outcome drastically raised the stakes for international maritime trade, causing further diversions of shipping around the Cape of Good Hope. International condemnation was swift, demanding an end to the reckless attacks on non-combatant seafarers.

description: Houthi militants fired two anti-ship ballistic missiles at the Liberian-flagged container ship Pinocchio in the Red Sea, causing no damage.
news_context:
The strike attempt occurred shortly after the fatal attack on the True Confidence, underlining the persistent threat to shipping despite continuous coalition airstrikes. The targeted vessel was reportedly linked to Oaktree Capital Management, loosely tying it to US financial interests.

The unabated attacks continued to force major shipping conglomerates to route vessels around Africa, significantly increasing global freight costs and delivery times. US Central Command confirmed the attack and subsequently conducted strikes destroying Houthi underwater drones.

description: Pro-Ukrainian Russian militias launched mechanized cross-border assaults into the Belgorod and Kursk regions, engaging in heavy firefights.
news_context:
The coordinated incursions utilized tanks and armored vehicles, forcing Russian border guards and local authorities to engage in heavy firefights to repel the advance. The militias claimed to have taken control of the village of Tetkino in the Kursk region.

The raids were strategically timed to coincide with the lead-up to the Russian presidential elections, aiming to embarrass Vladimir Putin and demonstrate that the Kremlin could not secure its own borders. Russia claimed to have killed over 100 militants and destroyed multiple armored vehicles during the repulse.

description: An Israeli airstrike hit an UNRWA food distribution center in eastern Rafah, killing five people including a senior Hamas police commander.
news_context:
Israel claimed the strike precisely targeted Mohammad Abu Hasna, whom they identified as a Hamas operative attempting to hijack humanitarian aid logistics. UNRWA confirmed the facility was one of its few remaining operational warehouses in the devastated enclave.

The strike further complicated the desperate humanitarian aid distribution network in Gaza, drawing sharp condemnation from UN officials. It highlighted the fraught environment where Israeli forces continually targeted civilian police and administrative personnel associated with the Hamas-run government.

description: Russian forces launched two Iskander-M ballistic missiles at Odesa in a double-tap attack, striking a residential area and then first responders.
news_context:
The deliberate use of a delayed second strike aimed to maximize casualties among medics, rescue workers, and police responding to the initial impact. This tactic is considered a grave violation of international humanitarian law and has been frequently employed by Russian forces in Ukraine and Syria.

The attack was one of the deadliest single strikes on Odesa since the start of the full-scale invasion. It drew widespread international condemnation and reinforced Ukrainian appeals for Patriot missile systems capable of intercepting fast-moving ballistic targets.
